steps.title: Spot the existing dash
steps.content: The original sentence already contains an em-dash before was released in 1970: ...for teaching programming skills—was released in 1970. Whatever fills the blank must pair with that closing em-dash.

steps.title: Pair the dashes
steps.content:
Em-dashes used as parenthetical marks must come in matching pairs. Choice A opens with an em-dash after Pascal: Pascal—created by Niklaus Wirth, which pairs with the existing closing dash to set off the non-essential information cleanly: Pascal—created by Niklaus Wirth—for teaching programming skills—was released in 1970.

Choices B and D mix commas with the existing em-dash, breaking the symmetry. Choice C omits punctuation entirely.

The answer is A.
